args: wt's help-dump command only emits each cobra command's canonical Name(), never its Aliases() -- so alias subcommands like 'ls' (alias for list), 'new' (alias for create), and 'rm' (alias for delete) don't appear in the generated help/wt.json, causing shll.ai's README-drift checker to flag 'wt ls', 'wt new', 'wt rm' from the README as unknown/nonexistent commands. Fix: extend help-dump's tree-walk to also emit registered aliases for each command node so alias-form invocations are discoverable in the dump.
